title: 5 Checks When Prayer Feels Unanswered
hook: Before you lose heart, run your prayer through these five checks.
script: Before you lose heart, run your prayer through these five checks. One, motive: am I asking to spend it on myself? James 4:3. Two, reconciliation: are my relationships hindered? 1 Peter 3:7; Matthew 5:23-24. Three, persistence: have I stopped knocking? Luke 18:1-8. Four, timing and unseen opposition: is there delay, not denial? Daniel 10:12-13. Five, God’s will: does my request align with His heart? 1 John 5:14-15. If one needs adjusting, God isn’t shaming you—He’s guiding you. Keep praying, keep aligning. He is for you, Romans 8:28-32. Save this and pray it through again tonight.

title: A 60-Second Prayer Check
hook: If God feels silent right now, listen to this.
script: If God feels silent right now, listen to this. God, search my motive; if I’m asking for me, purify my heart (James 4:3). Bring to mind anyone I need to make peace with; I choose to forgive and reach out (1 Peter 3:7; Matthew 5:23-24). Give me persistence like the widow who kept knocking (Luke 18:1-8). I trust Your timing, even in battles I can’t see (Daniel 10:12-13). Align me with Your will; if You say yes, amen; if You say wait, I’ll worship (1 John 5:14-15). And remind me You are for me in Christ (Romans 8:28-32). Replay this prayer whenever silence feels loud.

title: You’re Not Ignored—You’re Being Shaped
hook: This truth can change how you see unanswered prayer.
script: This truth can change how you see unanswered prayer. Years ago I thought heaven was quiet, until someone handed me five simple checks. Is my motive about my kingdom or God’s? James 4:3. Is there a call I need to make to reconcile? Matthew 5:23-24; 1 Peter 3:7. Am I quitting too soon? Luke 18:1-8. Could timing or spiritual warfare be slowing the delivery? Daniel 10:12-13. Does this request match His will revealed in Scripture? 1 John 5:14-15. I adjusted, stayed faithful, and peace returned before the answer did. God did not spare His own Son. He’s for you, Romans 8:32. Keep knocking. Save this for tomorrow’s prayer.
